'''Muizz Mustapha''' (born 3 April 2000) is a [[Nigeria]]n professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has previously played for [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan and dual registration at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Super League, at [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Dewsbury Rams]], [[Bradford Bulls]] and [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]], and at [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

== Background ==
Mustapha was born in [[Nigeria]].<ref>{{cite web |date=4 September 2019 |title=Meet Elliot Wallis - The explosive Hull KR winger called up by new Nigerian national team |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/meet-elliot-wallis-explosive-hull-19437350 |accessdate=1 November 2019 |publisher=Mirror}}</ref> He moved to [[Leeds]] with his family as a young child, initially settling in [[Harehills]] then later [[Hunslet]].<ref name="LL Leeds future">{{Cite web |last=Heppenstall |first=Ross |date=26 July 2022 |title=Muizz Mustapha discusses Leeds Rhinos future after living a 'dream come true' |url=https://www.leeds-live.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/muizz-mustapha-leeds-rhinos-future-24588094 |access-date=23 December 2023 |website=Leeds Live}}</ref>

Mustapha played junior rugby league for Hunslet Warriors. He joined the [[Leeds Rhinos]] youth system at scholarship level.<ref name="LL Leeds future" />

== Career ==

=== Leeds Rhinos ===
In 2018, he made a professional debut while on loan at [[Featherstone Rovers]] and has also spent time at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

In 2019, he made debut for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] against [[Workington Town]] in the [[Challenge Cup]].<ref name="BBC Leeds v Workington">{{cite web |date=12 April 2019 |title=Challenge Cup: Leeds Rhinos 78-6 Workington Town |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3018145 |accessdate=13 April 2019 |publisher=BBC}}</ref>

On 18 November 2020, it was announced that Mustapha would join [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a season-long loan starting in 2021.<ref>{{cite web |title=Leeds forward Muizz Mustapha joins Hull KR on loan after extending Rhinos stay |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-forward-muizz-mustapha-joins-hull-kr-on-loan-after-extending-rhinos-stay/ |accessdate=15 Dec 2020 |website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

==== Hull Kingston Rovers (loan) ====
Mustapha made a total of eleven appearances for Hull KR in the [[2021 Hull Kingston Rovers season|2021 season]] including the club's 28–10 semi-final defeat against [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/ |website=[[The Guardian]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/ |website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 0-19 Hull KR - Robins set up a semi-final at Catalans |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58663060.amp/ |website=[[BBC]]}}</ref>

==== Bradford Bulls (loan) ====
On 2 December 2021, it was announced that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web |date=2 Dec 2021 |title=Leeds loan three players to Bradford |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-loan-three-players-to-bradford/ |accessdate=2 Dec 2021 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 3 October 2022, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Mustapha from Leeds.<ref>{{Cite web |date=3 October 2022 |title=Muizz Mustapha: Castleford Tigers sign prop on one-year deal from Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63123216 |access-date=23 December 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He would join on a one-year deal, with an option for a further year in the club's favour.<ref>{{cite web |last=McKenna |first=Ben |date=3 October 2022 |title=Castleford Tigers sign forward Muizz Mustapha from Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-sign-forward-muizz-mustapha-from-leeds-rhinos-3865888/ |access-date=23 December 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> Head coach [[Lee Radford]] said, "His leg speed, his defence, his line speed is fantastic, and he is a different shape to a lot of front rowers kicking around in the competition."<ref>{{Cite web |date=3 October 2022 |title=Muizz Mustapha moves to the Tigers |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8317 |access-date=23 December 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

Mustapha made his Castleford debut on 26 February 2023 against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cartwright |first=Phil |date=26 February 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 6-24 St Helens - club world champions claim win in domestic opener |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64721685 |access-date=23 December 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He played thirteen matches for Castleford in the [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|2023 season]] as the club finished 11th on the table narrowly avoiding relegation.<ref>{{cite web |title=Castleford Tigers ace Alex Mellor delivers brutally honest assessment of 'awful' season; 'We've massively underperformed' |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively/ |website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

In the 2025 season, he made seventeen appearances for Castleford, scoring three tries.<ref name="RLRKC" /> On 10 October, Castleford announced Mustapha would depart the club to pursue playing opportunities elsewhere.<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 October 2025 |title=Prop Mustapha released by Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/czjvrzpx4wgo |access-date=11 October 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Oct 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Castleford forward enters open market as Tigers confirm shock release|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-confirm-shock-muizz-mustapha-release}}</ref>

====Batley Bulldogs (DR)====
In June 2024, Mustapha made two appearances for [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]], through their dual registration agreement with Castleford.<ref name="RLRKC" />

=== Doncaster R.L.F.C. ===
On 26 October 2025, Mustapha signed for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=26 Oct 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Released Castleford Tigers prop lands new club as length of Championship deal confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/muizz-mustapha-joins-doncaster-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>

== International ==
On 11 September 2019, Mustapha was called up for the [[Nigeria national rugby league team|Nigeria]] 60-man training squad.<ref>{{cite web |date=11 Sep 2019 |title=60 Man Nigeria Squad |url=https://nigeriarugbyleague.org/2019/09/11/60-man-nigeria-squad/ |accessdate=24 Dec 2020 |website=www.nigeriarugbyleague.org}}</ref>

He played for [[Nigeria national rugby league team|Nigeria]] when they faced off against a combined [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]] side in [[Barrow-in-Furness|Barrow]] in November 2025, he started at loose forward and scored Nigeria's only try in the 70-6 loss.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Cumbria score 13 tries as Nigeria overrun – Total Rugby League |url=https://www.totalrl.com/cumbria-score-13-tries-as-nigeria-overrun/ |access-date=2026-04-09 |language=en-US}}</ref>
